def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

if push_to_hub and "/" in save_directory:

        # +1 solves absolute path issues
        new_save_directory = save_directory
        username = new_save_directory[:new_save_directory.find("/")]
        new_save_directory = new_save_directory[new_save_directory.find("/")+1:]
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            new_save_directory = os.path.join(KAGGLE_TMP, new_save_directory[new_save_directory.find("/")+1:])
            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: You are pushing to hub in Kaggle environment.\n"\
                f"To save memory, we shall move {save_directory} to {new_save_directory}"
            )
        else:
            logger.warning_once(
                f"Unsloth: You are pushing to hub, but you passed your HF username = {username}.\n"\
                f"We shall truncate {save_directory} to {new_save_directory}"
            )

        save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"] = new_save_directory
        tokenizer_save_settings ["save_directory"] = new_save_directory
        save_directory = new_save_directory
    pass

    print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 16bit...")

    # Determine max RAM usage minus sharding
    max_ram = psutil.virtual_memory().available
    sharded_ram_usage = 5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024
    if type(max_shard_size) is str:
        gb_found = re.match(r"([0-9]{1,})[\s]{0,}GB", max_shard_size, flags = re.IGNORECASE)
        mb_found = re.match(r"([0-9]{1,})[\s]{0,}MB", max_shard_size, flags = re.IGNORECASE)
        if   gb_found: sharded_ram_usage = int(gb_found.group(1)) * 1024 * 1024 * 1024
        elif mb_found: sharded_ram_usage = int(mb_found.group(1)) * 1024 * 1024
    elif type(max_shard_size) is int:
        sharded_ram_usage = sharded_ram_usage
    pass

    # Switch to our fast saving modules if it's a slow PC!
    n_cpus = psutil.cpu_count(logical = False)
    if n_cpus is None: n_cpus = psutil.cpu_count()
    if n_cpus is None: n_cpus = 1

    if safe_serialization is None:
        safe_serialization = True
        save_pretrained_settings["safe_serialization"] = safe_serialization

    elif safe_serialization and (n_cpus <= 2):
        logger.warning_once(
            f"Unsloth: You have {n_cpus} CPUs. Using `safe_serialization` is 10x slower.\n"\
            f"We shall switch to Pytorch saving, which might take 3 minutes and not 30 minutes.\n"\
            f"To force `safe_serialization`, set it to `None` instead.",
        )
        safe_serialization = False
        save_function = fast_save_pickle
        save_pretrained_settings["safe_serialization"] = safe_serialization
        save_pretrained_settings["save_function"]      = save_function
    pass

    # Only safe_serialization uses more RAM
    if safe_serialization:
        max_ram -= sharded_ram_usage
    else:
        max_ram -= sharded_ram_usage*0.25 # Uses much less
    pass

    max_ram = int(max(0, max_ram) * maximum_memory_usage)
    print(f"Unsloth: Will use up to "\
          f"{round(max_ram/1024/1024/1024, 2)} out of "\
          f"{round(psutil.virtual_memory().total/1024/1024/1024, 2)} RAM for saving.")

    # Move temporary_location to /tmp in Kaggle
    if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
        temporary_location = os.path.join(KAGGLE_TMP, temporary_location)

    # Max directory for disk saving
    if not os.path.exists(temporary_location):
        os.makedirs(temporary_location)
    pass

    # Check if Kaggle or Colab, since only 20GB of Disk space allowed.
    if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT or IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT:
        # We free up 4GB of space
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: Kaggle/Colab has limited disk space. We need to delete the downloaded\n"\
            "model which will save 4-16GB of disk space, allowing you to save on Kaggle/Colab."
        )
        _free_cached_model(internal_model)
    pass

    # HF also uses a OrderedDict
    from collections import OrderedDict

    torch_dtype = dtype_from_config(internal_model.config)
    if type(torch_dtype) is str:
        if   torch_dtype ==  "float16": torch_dtype = torch.float16
        elif torch_dtype == "bfloat16": torch_dtype = torch.bfloat16

if Trainer._inner_training_loop.__name__ != "_fast_inner_training_loop":
            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Unsuccessfully patched Trainer! Please file a bug report!")
        pass

        # Fix loftq issues
        # loftq_config must not = None, but rather {}
        all_configs = model.peft_config
        for key, current_config in all_configs.items():
            if hasattr(current_config, "loftq_config") and current_config.loftq_config is None:
                new_args = current_config.__dict__
                new_args["loftq_config"] = {}
                current_config = current_config.__class__(**new_args)
                all_configs[key] = current_config
            pass
        pass

        # Do patching
        n_mlp = 0
        n_qkv = 0
        n_o   = 0

        active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0] if \
            hasattr(model, "active_adapters") else model.active_adapter

        # Get dropout and bias
        lora_dropout = model.peft_config[active_adapter].lora_dropout
        bias         = model.peft_config[active_adapter].bias

Now I have all the information I need. Let me now design the implementation plan. The key things I've learned:

1. **LoRA config access**: `model.peft_config["default"]` gives us a PEFT LoraConfig with `.r`, `.lora_alpha`, `.target_modules`
2. **LoRA weight access**: Through `get_lora_parameters()` or `get_lora_parameters_bias()` in `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` — accesses `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` and `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`


3. **Method attachment pattern**: Methods are added to the model in `patch_saving_functions()` via `types.MethodType()`
4. **Layer iteration**: Iterate `internal_model.model.layers` with `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` to get projections
5. **File**: All save methods are in `unsloth/save.py`
6. **Exports**: `__all__` in `save.py` and `from .save import *` in `__init__.py`

Now I'm thinking through the ONNX export implementation. The method needs to export the LoRA adapter weights—both the A and B matrices—along with the configuration parameters.

I'm considering two approaches: using `torch.onnx.export` to wrap each LoRA pair in a simple module, or building ONNX graphs directly with the `onnx` library. The first approach seems cleaner and more maintainable, so I'll go with creating wrapper modules for each LoRA adapter pair and exporting them through PyTorch's ONNX export functionality. I'm realizing the practical challenge here — different LoRA layers have different matrix dimensions, so bundling them into a single ONNX model gets messy. The cleaner approach is to export each LoRA adapter pair as its own ONNX file, or use the ONNX library directly to construct a model with all the weights as initializers rather than trying to wrap them in PyTorch modules. I'm settling on the most practical approach: create a single ONNX file per LoRA adapter that encodes the A and B matrices along with the scaling factor, performing the computation `output = (input @ A^T) @ B^T * s`. This way, consumers can load the ONNX model and directly apply the LoRA delta to their base model outputs. I'm settling on the `torch.onnx.export` approach since it's the standard method and doesn't require additional dependencies, then I'll wrap each LoRA module, export it with metadata, and save everything organized in the output directory with a JSON file tracking the rank, alpha, and target modules. Now I'm iterating through each layer to extract the LoRA parameters using the same approach as the save function, then wrapping each extracted LoRA component in a module and exporting it to ONNX format with appropriate dummy inputs.

After exporting, I'll load each ONNX file and attach metadata like the rank, alpha value, and target module name directly to the model, then save the updated files. Finally, I'm creating a summary JSON file that captures the overall LoRA configuration including rank and alpha.

Now I'm reconsidering whether to export separate ONNX files per layer or consolidate everything into a single model. The multi-file approach mirrors how safetensors handles LoRA exports with named keys, which is more flexible, but a single unified file could also work depending on what the user expects.

I think the best approach is to create individual ONNX subgraphs for each LoRA adapter pair and organize them in a structured directory alongside metadata JSON. This gives users the flexibility to load and integrate these components into their own ONNX inference pipelines, and it's consistent with how LoRA adapters are typically distributed.

For the implementation, I'll use `torch.onnx.export` with a simple wrapper module that holds the A and B weight matrices as parameters. This is the standard approach and avoids adding extra dependencies since unsloth already depends on torch.

Defining the wrapper module... I'm laying out the complete directory structure for the exported LoRA layers, organizing them by model layer with individual ONNX files for each adapted projection and a metadata file at the root to track configuration details like rank and alpha values.

Actually, creating that many individual files might be unwieldy—I should reconsider the export strategy. A single consolidated ONNX file would be cleaner, but that requires some workarounds since ONNX isn't designed as a weight container. The better compromise is a flat directory structure where each LoRA adapter gets its own ONNX file with a descriptive naming scheme, paired with a single metadata JSON that tracks the overall configuration. I'm realizing I should focus on what's actually practical here. The standard approach is to export LoRA adapters as standalone ONNX models that can be applied during inference, with one file per adapted layer to keep things manageable. But creating hundreds of files seems unwieldy, so I'm going to simplify and just build a single ONNX file that contains all the LoRA weights and can apply the transformations in one go. I'm settling on a per-layer ONNX approach instead—each layer gets a single file containing all its LoRA computations (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, etc.), which reduces 224 files down to just 32 for a typical model. The forward pass takes multiple hidden states as inputs and returns multiple delta tensors, keeping everything modular while staying practical.

Defining the layer adapter class... I'm applying the scaling factor to the output and returning all the results as a tuple. Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this—the cleanest approach is to create one ONNX file per LoRA-adapted module with a simple input-to-output transformation, paired with a metadata JSON file that stores the global configuration. This keeps each graph minimal and ONNX-idiomatic, and while it creates multiple files, they're all small since LoRA weights are compact. I'll use a clear naming convention to organize them in the output directory. I'm settling on the per-module ONNX files with a metadata JSON approach since it integrates cleanly into ONNX pipelines. But I need to handle a few edge cases: checking whether each module actually has LoRA adapters before trying to export them, accounting for the PeftModel wrapper layers, and properly accessing the underlying model structure through the wrapper.

def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

    if commit_description is None:
        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\

        )

        pass

        # Update model tag

        (

            revision           = revision,
            commit_description = commit_description,

            # Set padding side to left for inference
            old_padding_side = tokenizer.padding_side
            tokenizer.padding_side = "left"

            getattr(tokenizer, "original_push_to_hub", tokenizer.push_to_hub)\
            (
                repo_id            = save_directory,
                use_temp_dir       = use_temp_dir,
                commit_message     = commit_message,
                private            = private,
                token              = token,
                max_shard_size     = max_shard_size,
                create_pr          = create_pr,
                safe_serialization = safe_serialization,
                revision           = revision,
                commit_description = commit_description,
                tags               = tags,
            )

            # Revert back padding side
            tokenizer.padding_side = old_padding_side
        pass

        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            print(f"Saved {save_method} model to https://huggingface.co/" + save_directory)

{
        "save_directory"  : save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"],
        "legacy_format"   : None,
        "filename_prefix" : None,
        "push_to_hub"     : save_pretrained_settings["push_to_hub"],
        "private"         : save_pretrained_settings["private"],
        "token"           : save_pretrained_settings["token"],
    }

    # Check if PEFT Model or not - if yes, 3 levels. If not 2 levels.
    from peft import PeftModelForCausalLM
    if isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM):
        internal_model = model.model
    else:
        internal_model = model
    pass

    # Cannot be converted properly!
    if (save_method == "merged_4bit") or (save_method == "lora") or (
        not hasattr(model, "model") or \
        not hasattr(internal_model.model, "layers")
    ):
        # Do general saving
        # Edit save_pretrained_settings
        # [TODO] _create_repo has errors due to **kwargs getting accepted
        # commit_description does not seem to work?
        what_to_delete = ("use_temp_dir", "commit_message", "create_pr", "revision", "commit_description", "tags",) \
            if save_pretrained_settings["push_to_hub"] is False else \
            ("use_temp_dir", "create_pr", "revision", "tags", "commit_description",)
        for deletion in what_to_delete:
            del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
        pass
        if hasattr(model, "add_model_tags"):
            model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

        # Update model tag
        if push_to_hub:
             _ = upload_to_huggingface(
                model, save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"], token,
                "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
                old_username = None, private = private,
            )
        pass

        if tokenizer is not None:
            print("Unsloth: Saving tokenizer...", end = "")

            # Set padding side to left for inference
            old_padding_side = tokenizer.padding_side
            tokenizer.padding_side = "left"

            tokenizer.save_pretrained(**tokenizer_save_settings)

            # Revert back padding side
            tokenizer.padding_side = old_padding_side

            print(" Done.")
        else:
            print()

        print("Unsloth: Saving model...", end = "")
        if save_method != "lora": print(" This might take 10 minutes for Llama-7b...", end = "")

        # [TODO] Is this correct?
        if save_method == "lora":
            save_pretrained_settings["selected_adapters"] = None
        pass

        model.save_pretrained(**save_pretrained_settings)

        if push_to_hub and hasattr(model, "config"):
            print("Saved to https://huggingface.co/" + save_pretrained_settings["save_directory"])
        pass

        print(" Done.")
        return save_directory, None
    pass

    # If push_to_hub, we must remove the .../ part of a repo
    username = None

def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

def unsloth_generic_save(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

    merge_and_overwrite_lora(
        get_model_name,
        model                = model,
        tokenizer            = tokenizer,
        save_directory       = save_directory,
        push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
        private              = private,
        token                = token,
        save_method          = save_method,
        output_dtype         = None,
        low_disk_space_usage = True,
        use_temp_file        = False,
    )
    return
pass


def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = True
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

input.content:
# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` Method to Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth currently supports exporting models in several formats (merged HF safetensors, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO) but has **no ONNX support**. This change adds an `export_to_onnx` method that exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) as standalone ONNX models. Each LoRA-adapted module becomes a small ONNX graph representing the delta computation `output = (input @ A^T) @ B^T * scaling`, enabling integration into ONNX Runtime inference pipelines.

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `unsloth/save.py` | Add `_LoRALayer` helper class, `unsloth_export_to_onnx()` function, update `__all__`, update `patch_saving_functions()` |

That's it — a single file change, following the established patterns.

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add `_LoRALayer` helper class (~line 188, after `_merge_lora`)

A lightweight `nn.Module` wrapper that holds one LoRA adapter pair and computes the delta:

```python
class _LoRALayer(torch.nn.Module):
    """Wraps a single LoRA adapter pair for ONNX export."""
    def __init__(self, lora_A, lora_B, scaling):
        super().__init__()
        self.lora_A = torch.nn.Parameter(lora_A.detach().clone().float())
        self.lora_B = torch.nn.Parameter(lora_B.detach().clone().float())
        self.scaling = scaling

    def forward(self, x):
        return (x @ self.lora_A.t()) @ self.lora_B.t() * self.scaling
```

- `lora_A` shape: `(rank, input_dim)` — stored as `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`
- `lora_B` shape: `(output_dim, rank)` — stored as `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`
- `scaling`: float scalar = `lora_alpha / rank`

### Step 2: Add `unsloth_export_to_onnx()` function (~line 2562, before `not_implemented_save`)

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    output_directory     : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version        : int = 17,
):
```

**Algorithm:**

1. **Unwrap PeftModel**: Same pattern as existing save methods (lines 389-395):
   ```python
   from peft import PeftModelForCausalLM
   if isinstance(self, PeftModelForCausalLM):
       internal_model = self.model
   else:
       internal_model = self
   ```

2. **Extract LoRA config** from `self.peft_config`:
   ```python
   active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0] if hasattr(self, "active_adapters") else "default"
   peft_cfg = self.peft_config[active_adapter]
   rank = peft_cfg.r
   lora_alpha = peft_cfg.lora_alpha
   target_modules = list(peft_cfg.target_modules)
   ```

3. **Create output directory**: `os.makedirs(output_directory, exist_ok=True)`

4. **Iterate layers and export** (reuses existing `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` constant and `get_lora_parameters` utility):
   ```python
   from .kernels import get_lora_parameters
   exported_files = []
   for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
       for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:
           proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
           W, quant_state, A, B, s = get_lora_parameters(proj)
           if A is None:
               continue
           lora_module = _LoRALayer(A, B, s)
           filename = f"layer_{j}_{item.replace('.', '_')}_lora.onnx"
           filepath = os.path.join(output_directory, filename)
           dummy_input = torch.randn(1, A.shape[1], dtype=torch.float32)
           torch.onnx.export(
               lora_module,
               dummy_input,
               filepath,
               opset_version=opset_version,
               input_names=["input"],
               output_names=["output"],
               dynamic_axes={"input": {0: "batch_size"}, "output": {0: "batch_size"}},
           )
           exported_files.append(filename)
   ```

5. **Optionally enrich ONNX metadata** (if `onnx` library is available):
   ```python
   try:
       import onnx
       for filename in exported_files:
           filepath = os.path.join(output_directory, filename)
           onnx_model = onnx.load(filepath)
           onnx_model.metadata_props.append(
               onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="lora_rank", value=str(rank)))
           onnx_model.metadata_props.append(
               onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="lora_alpha", value=str(lora_alpha)))
           onnx_model.metadata_props.append(
               onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="target_modules", value=",".join(target_modules)))
           onnx.save(onnx_model, filepath)
   except ImportError:
       pass  # onnx library optional for metadata embedding
   ```

6. **Save metadata JSON** (`adapter_config.json`) to output directory:
   ```python
   import json
   metadata = {
       "rank": rank,
       "lora_alpha": lora_alpha,
       "target_modules": target_modules,
       "opset_version": opset_version,
       "num_layers": len(internal_model.model.layers),
       "scaling": lora_alpha / rank,
       "adapter_files": exported_files,
   }
   with open(os.path.join(output_directory, "adapter_config.json"), "w") as f:
       json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)
   ```

7. **Print summary and clean up**:
   ```python
   print(f"Unsloth: Exported {len(exported_files)} LoRA adapters to ONNX in '{output_directory}'")
   for _ in range(3):
       gc.collect()
   ```

### Step 3: Register in `patch_saving_functions()` (line ~2665)

Add alongside existing method attachments:

```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

Add this in **both** the `if not vision:` branch (line 2658, inside `if hasattr(model, "config"):`) and the `else:` (vision) branch (line 2669).

### Step 4: Update `__all__` (line 48-54)

Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to the `__all__` list.

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| Per-module ONNX files | Each file is a clean, self-contained ONNX graph. Allows selective loading and maximum flexibility for consumers. |
| `torch.onnx.export` (not raw `onnx` API) | Standard PyTorch approach; no hard dependency on `onnx` library. |
| `onnx` library optional for metadata | Only used to embed metadata_props into ONNX files. Falls back gracefully. JSON metadata always saved. |
| Dynamic batch axis | `dynamic_axes={"input": {0: "batch_size"}}` allows variable batch sizes at inference. |
| Float32 export | LoRA weights exported in float32 for maximum precision and ONNX compatibility. |
| Reuses `get_lora_parameters()` | Existing utility from `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (line 190) handles adapter lookup, QAT, and edge cases. |
| Reuses `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` constant | Defined at line 67-70 in `save.py`, lists all target projection names. |

---

## Existing Utilities Reused

| Utility | File | Line | Purpose |
|---------|------|------|---------|
| `get_lora_parameters()` | `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | 190 | Extract A, B, scaling from PEFT linear layer |
| `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` | `unsloth/save.py` | 67 | Tuple of target module names to iterate |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | `unsloth/save.py` | 2569 | Method attachment point for all save/export methods |
| PeftModel unwrap pattern | `unsloth/save.py` | 389-395 | `isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM)` check |
| `model.peft_config[adapter]` | PEFT library | — | Access LoRA config (r, lora_alpha, target_modules) |

---

## Output Directory Structure

```
output_directory/
├── adapter_config.json                          # Metadata (rank, alpha, modules, file list)
├── layer_0_self_attn_q_proj_lora.onnx          # LoRA delta for layer 0 Q projection
├── layer_0_self_attn_k_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_self_attn_v_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_self_attn_o_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_mlp_gate_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_mlp_up_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_mlp_down_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_1_self_attn_q_proj_lora.onnx
├── ...
└── layer_N_mlp_down_proj_lora.onnx
```

Each `.onnx` file contains:
- **Graph**: `input → MatMul(A^T) → MatMul(B^T) → Mul(scaling) → output`
- **Parameters**: `lora_A` and `lora_B` as ONNX initializers
- **Metadata** (if `onnx` lib available): `lora_rank`, `lora_alpha`, `target_modules`

---

## User API

```python
from unsloth import FastLanguageModel

model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, r=16, lora_alpha=16, ...)
# ... training ...

# New method — exposed alongside existing save/export methods:
model.export_to_onnx("./my_lora_onnx/")
model.export_to_onnx("./my_lora_onnx/", opset_version=14)
```

---

## Verification

1. **Syntax check**: `python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('unsloth/save.py').read())"` — ensures no syntax errors
2. **Import check**: `python -c "from unsloth.save import unsloth_export_to_onnx"` — verifies export
3. **Inspect `patch_saving_functions`**: Confirm `export_to_onnx` is attached alongside existing methods
4. **Unit test** (manual, requires model): Load a LoRA-finetuned model, call `model.export_to_onnx("test_output/")`, verify:
   - `adapter_config.json` exists and contains correct metadata
   - `.onnx` files exist for each layer × target module
   - Each ONNX file loads with `onnx.load()` and passes `onnx.checker.check_model()`
   - ONNX model input/output shapes match expected dimensions

| Reuses `get_lora_parameters()` | Existing utility from `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (line 190) handles adapter lookup, QAT, and edge cases. |
| Reuses `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` constant | Defined at line 67-70 in `save.py`, lists all target projection names. |

---

```
output_directory/
├── adapter_config.json                          # Metadata (rank, alpha, modules, file list)
├── layer_0_self_attn_q_proj_lora.onnx          # LoRA delta for layer 0 Q projection
├── layer_0_self_attn_k_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_self_attn_v_proj_lora.onnx

├── ...

```

input.plan:
# Plan: Add `export_to_onnx` Method to Unsloth

## Context

Unsloth currently supports exporting models in several formats (merged HF safetensors, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO) but has **no ONNX support**. This change adds an `export_to_onnx` method that exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) as standalone ONNX models. Each LoRA-adapted module becomes a small ONNX graph representing the delta computation `output = (input @ A^T) @ B^T * scaling`, enabling integration into ONNX Runtime inference pipelines.

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `unsloth/save.py` | Add `_LoRALayer` helper class, `unsloth_export_to_onnx()` function, update `__all__`, update `patch_saving_functions()` |

That's it — a single file change, following the established patterns.

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add `_LoRALayer` helper class (~line 188, after `_merge_lora`)

A lightweight `nn.Module` wrapper that holds one LoRA adapter pair and computes the delta:

```python
class _LoRALayer(torch.nn.Module):
    """Wraps a single LoRA adapter pair for ONNX export."""
    def __init__(self, lora_A, lora_B, scaling):
        super().__init__()
        self.lora_A = torch.nn.Parameter(lora_A.detach().clone().float())
        self.lora_B = torch.nn.Parameter(lora_B.detach().clone().float())
        self.scaling = scaling

    def forward(self, x):
        return (x @ self.lora_A.t()) @ self.lora_B.t() * self.scaling
```

- `lora_A` shape: `(rank, input_dim)` — stored as `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`
- `lora_B` shape: `(output_dim, rank)` — stored as `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`
- `scaling`: float scalar = `lora_alpha / rank`

### Step 2: Add `unsloth_export_to_onnx()` function (~line 2562, before `not_implemented_save`)

```python
@torch.inference_mode()
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    output_directory     : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version        : int = 17,
):
```

**Algorithm:**

1. **Unwrap PeftModel**: Same pattern as existing save methods (lines 389-395):
   ```python
   from peft import PeftModelForCausalLM
   if isinstance(self, PeftModelForCausalLM):
       internal_model = self.model
   else:
       internal_model = self
   ```

2. **Extract LoRA config** from `self.peft_config`:
   ```python
   active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0] if hasattr(self, "active_adapters") else "default"
   peft_cfg = self.peft_config[active_adapter]
   rank = peft_cfg.r
   lora_alpha = peft_cfg.lora_alpha
   target_modules = list(peft_cfg.target_modules)
   ```

3. **Create output directory**: `os.makedirs(output_directory, exist_ok=True)`

4. **Iterate layers and export** (reuses existing `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` constant and `get_lora_parameters` utility):
   ```python
   from .kernels import get_lora_parameters
   exported_files = []
   for j, layer in enumerate(internal_model.model.layers):
       for item in LLAMA_WEIGHTS:
           proj = eval(f"layer.{item}")
           W, quant_state, A, B, s = get_lora_parameters(proj)
           if A is None:
               continue
           lora_module = _LoRALayer(A, B, s)
           filename = f"layer_{j}_{item.replace('.', '_')}_lora.onnx"
           filepath = os.path.join(output_directory, filename)
           dummy_input = torch.randn(1, A.shape[1], dtype=torch.float32)
           torch.onnx.export(
               lora_module,
               dummy_input,
               filepath,
               opset_version=opset_version,
               input_names=["input"],
               output_names=["output"],
               dynamic_axes={"input": {0: "batch_size"}, "output": {0: "batch_size"}},
           )
           exported_files.append(filename)
   ```

5. **Optionally enrich ONNX metadata** (if `onnx` library is available):
   ```python
   try:
       import onnx
       for filename in exported_files:
           filepath = os.path.join(output_directory, filename)
           onnx_model = onnx.load(filepath)
           onnx_model.metadata_props.append(
               onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="lora_rank", value=str(rank)))
           onnx_model.metadata_props.append(
               onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="lora_alpha", value=str(lora_alpha)))
           onnx_model.metadata_props.append(
               onnx.StringStringEntryProto(key="target_modules", value=",".join(target_modules)))
           onnx.save(onnx_model, filepath)
   except ImportError:
       pass  # onnx library optional for metadata embedding
   ```

6. **Save metadata JSON** (`adapter_config.json`) to output directory:
   ```python
   import json
   metadata = {
       "rank": rank,
       "lora_alpha": lora_alpha,
       "target_modules": target_modules,
       "opset_version": opset_version,
       "num_layers": len(internal_model.model.layers),
       "scaling": lora_alpha / rank,
       "adapter_files": exported_files,
   }
   with open(os.path.join(output_directory, "adapter_config.json"), "w") as f:
       json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)
   ```

7. **Print summary and clean up**:
   ```python
   print(f"Unsloth: Exported {len(exported_files)} LoRA adapters to ONNX in '{output_directory}'")
   for _ in range(3):
       gc.collect()
   ```

### Step 3: Register in `patch_saving_functions()` (line ~2665)

Add alongside existing method attachments:

```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

Add this in **both** the `if not vision:` branch (line 2658, inside `if hasattr(model, "config"):`) and the `else:` (vision) branch (line 2669).

### Step 4: Update `__all__` (line 48-54)

Add `"unsloth_export_to_onnx"` to the `__all__` list.

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| Per-module ONNX files | Each file is a clean, self-contained ONNX graph. Allows selective loading and maximum flexibility for consumers. |
| `torch.onnx.export` (not raw `onnx` API) | Standard PyTorch approach; no hard dependency on `onnx` library. |
| `onnx` library optional for metadata | Only used to embed metadata_props into ONNX files. Falls back gracefully. JSON metadata always saved. |
| Dynamic batch axis | `dynamic_axes={"input": {0: "batch_size"}}` allows variable batch sizes at inference. |
| Float32 export | LoRA weights exported in float32 for maximum precision and ONNX compatibility. |
| Reuses `get_lora_parameters()` | Existing utility from `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (line 190) handles adapter lookup, QAT, and edge cases. |
| Reuses `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` constant | Defined at line 67-70 in `save.py`, lists all target projection names. |

---

## Existing Utilities Reused

| Utility | File | Line | Purpose |
|---------|------|------|---------|
| `get_lora_parameters()` | `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | 190 | Extract A, B, scaling from PEFT linear layer |
| `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` | `unsloth/save.py` | 67 | Tuple of target module names to iterate |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | `unsloth/save.py` | 2569 | Method attachment point for all save/export methods |
| PeftModel unwrap pattern | `unsloth/save.py` | 389-395 | `isinstance(model, PeftModelForCausalLM)` check |
| `model.peft_config[adapter]` | PEFT library | — | Access LoRA config (r, lora_alpha, target_modules) |

---

## Output Directory Structure

```
output_directory/
├── adapter_config.json                          # Metadata (rank, alpha, modules, file list)
├── layer_0_self_attn_q_proj_lora.onnx          # LoRA delta for layer 0 Q projection
├── layer_0_self_attn_k_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_self_attn_v_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_self_attn_o_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_mlp_gate_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_mlp_up_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_0_mlp_down_proj_lora.onnx
├── layer_1_self_attn_q_proj_lora.onnx
├── ...
└── layer_N_mlp_down_proj_lora.onnx
```

Each `.onnx` file contains:
- **Graph**: `input → MatMul(A^T) → MatMul(B^T) → Mul(scaling) → output`
- **Parameters**: `lora_A` and `lora_B` as ONNX initializers
- **Metadata** (if `onnx` lib available): `lora_rank`, `lora_alpha`, `target_modules`

---

## User API

```python

model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(model, r=16, lora_alpha=16, ...)
# ... training ...

# New method — exposed alongside existing save/export methods:
model.export_to_onnx("./my_lora_onnx/")
model.export_to_onnx("./my_lora_onnx/", opset_version=14)
```

---

## Verification

1. **Syntax check**: `python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('unsloth/save.py').read())"` — ensures no syntax errors
2. **Import check**: `python -c "from unsloth.save import unsloth_export_to_onnx"` — verifies export
3. **Inspect `patch_saving_functions`**: Confirm `export_to_onnx` is attached alongside existing methods
4. **Unit test** (manual, requires model): Load a LoRA-finetuned model, call `model.export_to_onnx("test_output/")`, verify:
   - `adapter_config.json` exists and contains correct metadata
   - `.onnx` files exist for each layer × target module
   - Each ONNX file loads with `onnx.load()` and passes `onnx.checker.check_model()`
   - ONNX model input/output shapes match expected dimensions

2581	    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")

2584	    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")
2585	
2586	    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
2587	    """
2588	    {docs}
2589	    """
2590	    arguments = dict(locals())
2591	    del arguments["self"]
2592	    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
2593	        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
2594	        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
2597	    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
2598	        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2599	
2600	    if "commit_message" in arguments:
2601	        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
2602	        if commit_message is not None:
2603	            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
2604	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
2605	                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
2606	        else:
2607	            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
2608	        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:
2611	        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
2612	        if commit_description is not None:
2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619	
2620	    # Update model tag
2621	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2622	        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
2623	            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
2624	            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
2625	            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
2626	        )
2627	    pass
2628	
2629	    try:
2630	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2631	    except:
2632	        del arguments["tags"]
2633	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2634	    pass
2635	
2636	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
2641	
2642	    original_model = model
2643	    while True:
2644	
2645	        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2646	            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
2647	            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)

11	def register_models():
12	    global _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED
13	
14	    if _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED:
15	        return
16	    _register_deepseek_models()
17	    _register_gemma_models()
18	    _register_llama_models()

25	def search_models(org: str = None, base_name: str = None, version: str = None, size: str = None, quant_types: list[QuantType] = None, search_pattern: str = None) -> list[ModelInfo]:
26	    """
27	    Get model info from the registry.
28	
29	    See registry.ModelInfo for more fields.
30	
31	    If search_pattern is provided, the full model path will be matched against the pattern, where the model path is the model_id on huggingface hub.
32	
33	    """
34	    if not _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED:
35	        register_models()
36	    
37	    model_infos = MODEL_REGISTRY.values()
38	    if org:
39	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.org == org]
40	    if base_name:
41	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.base_name == base_name]
42	    if version:
43	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.version == version]
44	    if size:
45	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.size == size]
46	    if quant_types:
47	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if any(model_info.quant_type == quant_type for quant_type in quant_types)]
48	    if search_pattern:
49	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if search_pattern in model_info.model_path]
50	    
51	    return model_infos

1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",

1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",

2414	    self,
2415	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2416	    tokenizer            = None,
2417	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
2418	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2419	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2420	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
2421	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
2422	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
2423	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",

2425	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
2426	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

2429	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
2430	):
2431	    """
2432	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
2433	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.
2434	
2435	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
2436	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
2437	        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
2438	        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
2439	    """

2443	            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
2444	        )
2445	    pass
2446	
2447	    arguments = dict(locals())
2448	    arguments["model"] = self
2449	    del arguments["self"]
2450	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2451	    for _ in range(3):
2452	        gc.collect()
2453	pass

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary report.

## Unsloth Codebase Exploration Report

Based on my thorough analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's a complete breakdown of its structure, save/export methods, and LoRA configuration handling:

### 1. MAIN PACKAGE STRUCTURE

**Top-level package**: `/unsloth/__init__.py`
- Imports and exposes the main public API
- Key imports:
  - `from .models import *` (models module)
  - `from .models import __version__` (version info)
  - `from .save import *` (save/export functions)
  - `from .chat_templates import *` (chat templates)
  - `from .tokenizer_utils import *` (tokenizer utilities)
  - `from .trainer import *` (trainer utilities)

**Main modules**:
- `/unsloth/models/` - Model loading and patching
- `/unsloth/save.py` - Save/export functionality (2678 lines)
- `/unsloth/kernels/` - Optimized CUDA kernels for LoRA

### 2. MAIN CLASSES AND ENTRY POINTS

### 3. LoRA ADAPTER CONFIGURATION

**`get_peft_model()` method signature** (line 2266):
```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r                   = 16,                    # LoRA rank
    target_modules      = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                           "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha          = 16,                    # LoRA alpha (scaling factor)
    lora_dropout        = 0.0,
    bias                = "none",
    layers_to_transform = None,
    layers_pattern      = None,
    use_gradient_checkpointing = "unsloth",
    random_state        = 3407,
    use_rslora          = False,
    modules_to_save     = None,
    init_lora_weights   = True,
    loftq_config        = {},
    temporary_location  = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    qat_scheme          = None,
    **kwargs,
):
```

**LoRA configuration storage**:
- Configuration is stored in `model.peft_config` dictionary (PEFT standard)
- Access via `model.peft_config["default"]` or specific adapter names
- Can be converted to dict via `.to_dict()` method
- Keys include: `r`, `lora_alpha`, `lora_dropout`, `bias`, `target_modules`, `modules_to_save`, `layers_to_transform`, `layers_pattern`, `use_rslora`, `init_lora_weights`, `loftq_config`

**Adapter management**:
- Access active adapters: `model.active_adapters` or `model.active_adapter`
- Multiple adapters can be registered in `model.peft_config.keys()`
- Base model name stored in: `model.peft_config[adapter_name].base_model_name_or_path`

```python
def unsloth_save_model(

    token = None,
    is_main_process = True,
    state_dict = None,
    save_function = torch.save,
    max_shard_size = "5GB",
    safe_serialization = True,
    variant = None,
    save_peft_format = True,
    use_temp_dir = None,
    commit_message = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private = None,
    create_pr = False,
    revision = None,
    commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags = None,
    temporary_location = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage = 0.9,
):
```

**Save methods exposed on model instances**:

1. **`save_pretrained_merged()`** (line 1321) - Save with merged LoRA weights

   - save_method options: "merged_16bit" (default), "merged_4bit", "lora"
   - Returns nothing, saves to disk

2. **`push_to_hub_merged()`** (line 1364) - Push merged model to Hugging Face Hub
   - Parameters: repo_id, tokenizer, save_method, use_temp_dir, commit_message, private, token, etc.

3. **`save_pretrained_gguf()`** (line 1768) - Save as GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible)
   - Parameters: save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method, first_conversion, push_to_hub, token, etc.
   - quantization_method options: "not_quantized", "fast_quantized", "quantized", "f32", "f16", "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q2_k", "q3_k_l", "q3_k_m", "q3_k_s", "q4_0", "q4_1", "q4_k_s", "q4_k", "q5_k", "q5_0", "q5_1", "q5_k_s", "q6_k"

4. **`push_to_hub_gguf()`** (line 1951) - Push GGUF format to Hub

5. **`save_pretrained_ggml()`** (line 2211) - Save as GGML format
   - Converts LoRA to GGML format

6. **`push_to_hub_ggml()`** (line 2143) - Push GGML format to Hub

7. **`save_pretrained_torchao()`** (line 2502) - Save with torchao quantization
   - Parameters: save_directory, tokenizer, torchao_config, push_to_hub, token

   - `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`

- `push_to_hub()` - Enhanced version with Unsloth tagging (line 2586)
  - Automatically adds "unsloth" tags
  - Wraps original HF method

### 5. HOW METHODS ARE PATCHED ONTO MODELS

**`patch_saving_functions()`** (line 2569):
- Dynamically patches model instances with custom save/export methods
- Creates method wrappers of type `types.MethodType` bound to the model
- Preserves original `push_to_hub` as `model.original_push_to_hub`
- Adds these methods to model:
  - `model.push_to_hub_merged`
  - `model.save_pretrained_merged`
  - `model.push_to_hub_gguf`
  - `model.save_pretrained_gguf`
  - `model.save_pretrained_torchao`
  - `model.push_to_hub_ggml`
  - `model.save_pretrained_ggml`
  - `model.push_to_hub` (wrapped version)

**Automatic patching**:
- Called automatically during model loading in `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()`
- Called after `get_peft_model()` to ensure methods are available

---

### 6. SAVE METHODS AND STRATEGIES

**Save method options** (parameter in most save functions):
1. **"lora"** - Save only LoRA adapters (minimal size)
2. **"merged_16bit"** - Merge LoRA into float16 weights (useful for GGUF/inference)
3. **"merged_4bit"** - Merge LoRA into int4 weights (useful for DPO/HF inference, smaller)

**Key saving workflow** (lines 283-500 in unsloth_save_model):
- Validates save method is valid
- For lora: saves with PEFT format (adapter_config.json, adapter_model.bin)
- For merged: calls merge_and_unload() if available
- Handles 4-bit dequantization for merged modes
- Saves quantization config appropriately

**Merge behavior** (line 299):
- Checks if model has `merge_and_unload()` method (from PEFT)
- Automatically calls it for merged save modes
- Handles QLoRA (4-bit) merging by dequantizing first

---

### 7. GGUF CONVERSION

**`save_to_gguf()` function** (line 952):
- Handles GGUF conversion via llama.cpp
- Supports multiple quantization methods
- Auto-installs llama.cpp if not present
- Converts HF format → GGUF 16bit → Quantized GGUF
- Returns list of created GGUF files

**Quantization methods** (line 79-103):
- Maps friendly names to actual GGUF quants
- Default: "fast_quantized" (q8_0)
- Supports IQ2/IQ3 and other advanced quants

---

### 8. HUB INTEGRATION

**Push to Hub mechanisms**:
- Wrapped `push_to_hub()` adds "unsloth" tags automatically
- `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True` handles authentication
- Uses HuggingFace Hub API via `huggingface_hub` library
- Supports repo creation, private repos, PRs, revisions

**Model card generation** (line 1410):
- Auto-generates model cards for uploaded models
- Includes training method and Unsloth attribution

---

### 9. CONFIGURATION FILES SAVED

When using `save_pretrained_merged()` or `save_pretrained_gguf()`, the following files are created:
- **config.json** - Model configuration
- **model.safetensors** or **.bin** - Model weights
- **adapter_config.json** (for lora only) - LoRA configuration
- **adapter_model.safetensors** (for lora only) - LoRA weights
- **tokenizer.model** or tokenizer files
- **tokenizer_config.json** - Tokenizer config
- **special_tokens_map.json** - Special tokens
- **generation_config.json** (if applicable)

For GGUF: `model.gguf` (and optionally multiple quantized versions)

---

### 10. KEY FILES FOR YOUR FEATURE

1. `/unsloth/save.py` - Main save logic (2678 lines)
   - Line 205: `unsloth_save_model()` - Core function

   - Line 2617: LoraConfig creation
   - Line 2649: PEFT model wrapping

3. `/unsloth/models/loader.py` - Model loading
   - Line 107: `FastLanguageModel` class
   - Line 109: `from_pretrained()` method

4. `/unsloth/models/__init__.py` - Public exports

5. `/unsloth/__init__.py` - Package-level exports

---

### 11. ACCESSING LoRA CONFIGURATION AT SAVE TIME

Once a model has LoRA applied:
```python
# Access current LoRA config:
peft_config_dict = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
# Contains: r, lora_alpha, target_modules, lora_dropout, bias, etc.

# Get rank:
rank = model.peft_config["default"].r

# Get alpha:
alpha = model.peft_config["default"].lora_alpha

# Get target modules:
target_modules = model.peft_config["default"].target_modules
```

This configuration is automatically saved in `adapter_config.json` when using "lora" save method.

---

### 12. TEST EXAMPLES

Location: `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`
- Shows how to call `save_pretrained_merged()` (line 127)
- Shows how to call `save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 214)
- Demonstrates save method variants: "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
- Tests tokenizer saving alongside model

---

This comprehensive structure shows that unsloth provides multiple export/save methods with flexibility for different use cases (LoRA-only, merged 16-bit, merged 4-bit, GGUF, TorchAO), all dynamically patched onto model instances. The LoRA configuration is stored in PEFT's standard `peft_config` format and can be easily accessed and modified at save time.

gc.collect()

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass
